prompt: A Japanese Edo-period ukiyo-e woodblock print in the style of traditional landscape masters such as Hokusai, depicting a lone traveler descending a steep mountain slope. The composition is strongly diagonal, guiding the eye from upper left to lower right. The traveler wears traditional Edo-era clothing and straw sandals, carefully stepping along a clearly defined, dominant stream of water flowing downhill. Several smaller, faint streams branch across the slope, but only one main stream is continuous and visually strongest, carving a clear path downward. The traveler deliberately follows this primary path while ignoring the others, emphasizing directional choice. Rendered with flat, unmodulated color planes, bold black ink outlines, and minimal shading. Visible woodblock print texture, slight ink bleed, and subtle color misregistration typical of hand-printed works. Stylized clouds and mist occupy negative space, with simplified natural forms and no perspective realism. Color palette restricted to indigo blue, muted green, beige, and soft gray. Strong figure-ground contrast, no gradients, no modern elements, no photorealism. The entire scene must resemble an authentic ukiyo-e print from the Edo period. Negative prompt: photorealistic, 3D rendering, modern clothing, text, labels, charts, gradients, soft shading, digital effects, hyper-detail, cinematic lighting
tradition_approach: Used diagonal composition, flat color planes, bold outlines, and stylized flowing water to mirror multiple gradients and the selection of the steepest descent path
